dose: 50 mg PO twice daily, 1 week on / 1 week off
change_magnitude: profound and selective reduction (figure-reported)
durability_described: Reduction sustained during metronomic schedule; recovery off-treatment
clinical_correlate: Restoration of T and NK effector functions
notes: Landmark Ghiringhelli 2007 metronomic-cyclo PD paper. Abstract-only extraction; numeric values are in figures/supplement, recorded as null. Side-list Ghiringhelli 2004 preclinical excluded from table.
title: Metronomic cyclophosphamide regimen selectively depletes CD4+CD25+ regulatory T cells and restores T and NK effector functions in end stage cancer patients

dose: Single IV dose (300 mg/m2 per paper)
patient_population: Metastatic cancer patients vs 24 healthy donors
change_magnitude: baseline 9.2% (patients) vs 7.1% (healthy) — post-cyclo: no significant modulation
notes: Audia 2007 — single-dose IV cyclo + BCG failed to modulate Treg numbers or function despite elevated baseline in cancer patients. Important null result anchoring the 'dose schedule matters' argument.
title: Increase of CD4+ CD25+ regulatory T cells in the peripheral blood of patients with metastatic carcinoma: a Phase I clinical trial using cyclophosphamide and immunotherapy to eliminate CD4+ CD25+ T lymphocytes

intervention: Denileukin diftitox (DAB389IL-2) prior to RCC tumor-RNA DC vaccine
combo_partners: Autologous RCC tumor-RNA dendritic cell vaccine
patient_population: Metastatic RCC patients receiving DC vaccine
timepoint_detail: pre- and post-denileukin diftitox; then post-vaccine
change_magnitude: significantly reduced (abstract); DAB389IL-2 selectively eliminated CD25+ Tregs
durability_described: Transient; permitted vaccine-induced tumor antigen response
notes: Dannull 2005 JCI — first clear demonstration that DAB389IL-2 depletes functional Tregs in cancer patients and enhances vaccine response. Numeric values in figures; abstract-derived.
title: Enhancement of vaccine-mediated antitumor immunity in cancer patients after depletion of regulatory T cells

change_magnitude: no elimination of Tregs observed
notes: Attia 2005 — key null result: DAB/IL-2 failed to eliminate Tregs in melanoma despite IL-2-receptor targeting rationale. Conflicts with Dannull 2005 (16308572); surface CD25 vs FOXP3 gating likely reconciles.
title: Inability of a fusion protein of IL-2 and diphtheria toxin (Denileukin Diftitox, DAB389IL-2, ONTAK) to eliminate regulatory T lymphocytes in patients with melanoma

change_magnitude: significantly reduced; successive doses further reduced
durability_described: Recovery observed over ~13 days after single dose; sustained with repeated dosing
clinical_correlate: Altered immune function in vivo/in vitro
notes: Mahnke 2007 — early human daclizumab Treg depletion kinetics paper. Concrete numbers in figures; abstract extraction only.
title: Depletion of CD4+CD25+ human regulatory T cells in vivo: kinetics of Treg depletion and alterations in immune functions in vivo and in vitro

intervention: Daclizumab (anti-CD25) prior to CEA-based cancer vaccine
timepoint_detail: pre- and post- multi-dose daclizumab; then post-vaccine
change_magnitude: direct evidence of depletion after multi-dose daclizumab
clinical_correlate: Enhanced CEA-specific T-cell responses to vaccine
notes: Morse 2008 Blood — pilot vaccine study. Multi-dose daclizumab did deplete; single-dose did not — schedule dependence noted.
title: Depletion of human regulatory T cells specifically enhances antigen-specific immune responses to cancer vaccines

change_magnitude: median 56.3% reduction from pre-DD to post-DD
clinical_correlate: Pilot efficacy with HD IL-2 combination
notes: Atchison 2010 pilot. Clean median % reduction reported in abstract.
title: A pilot study of denileukin diftitox (DD) in combination with high-dose interleukin-2 (IL-2) for patients with metastatic renal cell carcinoma

change_magnitude: depletion of CD4+CD25HIFoxp3+ Tregs reported
notes: Telang 2011 Phase II — DD monotherapy in melanoma. Abstract extraction.
title: Phase II trial of the regulatory T cell-depleting agent, denileukin diftitox, in patients with unresectable stage IV melanoma

intervention: Single-dose denileukin diftitox + gp100:209-217(210M) peptide vaccine
change_magnitude: single dose did not effectively deplete Tregs
clinical_correlate: No improvement in vaccine-induced T cell responses vs vaccine alone
notes: Luke 2016 RCT — single-dose denileukin diftitox failed where multi-dose succeeds (compare Morse 2008).
title: Single dose denileukin diftitox does not enhance vaccine-induced T cell responses or effectively deplete Tregs in advanced melanoma

change_magnitude: augmented depletion with IFNα
notes: Thibodeaux 2021 — IFNα enhances denileukin diftitox depletion efficacy in ovarian cancer.
title: IFNα Augments Clinical Efficacy of Regulatory T-cell Depletion with Denileukin Diftitox in Ovarian Cancer

change_magnitude: therapeutic reduction in cell-mediated immunosuppression reported
notes: Geskin 2018 — CTCL DD study extending CTCL PD profile.
title: Therapeutic reduction of cell-mediated immunosuppression in mycosis fungoides and Sézary syndrome

change_magnitude: decreased Tregs in peripheral blood
notes: Liao 2024 — IP route in ovarian. Same cohort with ascites compartment below.
title: Intraperitoneal immunotherapy with denileukin diftitox (ONTAK) in recurrent refractory ovarian cancer

change_magnitude: decreased Tregs in ascites
notes: Companion row to PBMC — ascites compartment Treg reduction (IP route).
title: Intraperitoneal immunotherapy with denileukin diftitox (ONTAK) in recurrent refractory ovarian cancer

dose: Per protocol, up to six 21-day cycles
change_magnitude: 40% of patients achieved ≥25% reduction in Tregs
notes: Gwin 2025 — partial depletion in breast cancer; subgroup responders.
title: A Phase II Study of Denileukin Diftitox in Patients with Advanced Treatment Refractory Breast Cancer

timepoint_detail: 2 weeks post-first dose; sustained during treatment
change_magnitude: eTreg depletion confirmed in most patients; sustained
clinical_correlate: Integrated phase 1a/1b; limited monotherapy clinical activity
notes: Fujikawa 2023 PLoS One — integrated phase 1a/1b. Canonical human moga Treg-PD readout in solid tumors.
title: Integrated analysis of phase 1a and 1b randomized controlled trials; Treg-targeted cancer immunotherapy with the humanized anti-CCR4 antibody, KW-0761, for advanced solid tumors

indication: Operable solid tumors (renal, lung, esophageal, oral SCC)
change_magnitude: CCR4+FOXP3+ T cells depleted in tumors of all 16 patients
notes: Jinushi 2025 JITC — first neoadjuvant moga+nivo study. Intratumoral CCR4+FOXP3+ depletion in all 16 patients.
title: Phase I study on neoadjuvant combination immunotherapy with mogamulizumab and nivolumab for solid tumors

change_magnitude: drastic decrease in activated Tregs within first 4 weeks
notes: Roelens 2022 — long-term moga PD in SS. Benign T cells also depleted (CCR4-broad).
title: Mogamulizumab induces long-term immune restoration and reshapes tumour heterogeneity in Sézary syndrome

change_magnitude: Long-term immune restoration; tumor heterogeneity reshaped
notes: Skin compartment of Roelens 2022 — same cohort, skin tissue readout.
title: Mogamulizumab induces long-term immune restoration and reshapes tumour heterogeneity in Sézary syndrome

change_magnitude: NK expansion and tumor cell reduction reported; Treg-specific numeric not in abstract
notes: Gordon 2025 — Treg-specific PD not detailed in abstract; row retained because moga class-effect depletion is expected. Will need full text for numeric Treg values.
title: A phase 1 study of interleukin-15 in combination with mogamulizumab in relapsed and refractory T-cell malignancies

change_magnitude: No difference in FOXP3 expression between responders and non-responders; CD8+ TIL up 1–100-fold in 14/18 patients
clinical_correlate: CD8 TIL increase independent of clinical response
notes: Huang 2011 — tremelimumab does not measurably change intratumoral FOXP3 despite CD8 infiltration. Classic early null-for-intratumoral-Treg-depletion result.
title: CTLA4 blockade induces frequent tumor infiltration by activated lymphocytes regardless of clinical responses in humans

change_magnitude: High baseline FoxP3 expression associated with clinical activity; TIL increase at week 3
change_significance: p=0.014 (baseline FoxP3 vs response); p=0.005 (TIL increase)
clinical_correlate: Higher baseline FoxP3 correlates with ipi response
notes: Hamid 2011 — intratumoral FOXP3 as predictive biomarker, not depletion endpoint. Design-level ambiguity: Treg density did not decrease post-ipi in this cohort.
title: A prospective phase II trial exploring the association between tumor microenvironment biomarkers and clinical activity of ipilimumab in advanced melanoma

tissue_detail: Stage-matched pre/post samples (melanoma n=19, prostate n=17, bladder n=9); mass cytometry on fresh melanoma n=5
change_magnitude: Both ipi and treme increase CD4+ and CD8+ infiltration without changing or depleting FOXP3+ cells
notes: Sharma 2019 — foundational human null result. Contrasts with mouse models showing ADCC-mediated intratumoral Treg depletion.
title: Anti-CTLA-4 Immunotherapy Does Not Deplete FOXP3+ Regulatory T Cells (Tregs) in Human Cancers

change_magnitude: Ipilimumab had little effect on circulating Treg frequencies
clinical_correlate: CD28-dependent T cell activation in blood
notes: Yi 2017 — ipi+chemo neoadj NSCLC, Treg unchanged in blood.
title: Immune Activation in Early-Stage Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Patients Receiving Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy Plus Ipilimumab

change_magnitude: Minimal impact on tumor Tregs reported
notes: Tumor-compartment row for Yi 2017. Small n (7) — meets ≥3 threshold.
title: Immune Activation in Early-Stage Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer Patients Receiving Neoadjuvant Chemotherapy Plus Ipilimumab

change_magnitude: No consistent PBMC Treg reduction; modest activation markers
notes: Comin-Anduix 2008 — detailed immunologic PBMC profile; no Treg depletion observed in blood.
title: Detailed analysis of immunologic effects of the cytotoxic T lymphocyte-associated antigen 4-blocking monoclonal antibody tremelimumab in peripheral blood of patients with melanoma

change_magnitude: No consistent FoxP3 reduction post-CTLA-4 blockade
notes: Ribas 2009 — intratumoral FoxP3/IDO in CTLA-4 blockade. Early null for Treg depletion in humans.
title: Intratumoral immune cell infiltrates, FoxP3, and indoleamine 2,3-dioxygenase in patients with melanoma undergoing CTLA4 blockade

change_magnitude: Profound long-lasting FoxP3+ Treg depletion in colonic mucosa
notes: Nancey 2012 — tissue-specific mucosal Treg depletion in ipi-enterocolitis. Intentional: irAE mechanism. Row uses 'other' for colonic mucosa.
title: Blockade of cytotoxic T-lymphocyte antigen-4 by ipilimumab is associated with a profound long-lasting depletion of Foxp3+ regulatory T cells

intervention: BMS-986218 (Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4) + androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) neoadjuvant
change_magnitude: TI-Treg reduction; correlates with CD16a/FCGR3A tumor macrophages
clinical_correlate: Depth of Treg inhibition and DC frequency associated with improved clinical outcomes
notes: Ager 2026 CRM — randomized phase 1. First human demonstration of Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 Treg depletion in prostate cancer. Preprint at 39314954.
title: Neoadjuvant Fc-enhanced anti-CTLA-4 targets Tregs to augment androgen deprivation in high-risk prostate cancer: A randomized phase I trial

change_magnitude: Selective intratumoral Treg depletion reported
notes: Chand 2024 Cancer Discovery — botensilimab first comprehensive human translational PD report.
title: Botensilimab, an Fc-Enhanced Anti-CTLA-4 Antibody, effective against tumors poorly responsive to conventional immunotherapy

change_magnitude: CD8/Treg ratio increased; absolute Treg expansion but skewed toward effectors
notes: Bentebibel 2019 — bempeg first-in-human; CAPTURED VIA RATIO-SHIFT PATH per user spec. Bempeg expands Tregs absolutely but shifts CD8/Treg.
title: A First-in-Human Study and Biomarker Analysis of NKTR-214, a Novel IL2Rβγ-Biased Cytokine, in Patients with Advanced or Metastatic Solid Tumors

indication: Advanced solid tumors (melanoma, RCC, NSCLC, UC)
change_magnitude: CD8/Treg ratio shift; Treg absolute expansion
notes: Diab 2020 PIVOT-02 — bempeg+nivo; ratio-shift framing per user spec.
title: Bempegaldesleukin (NKTR-214) plus Nivolumab in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors: Phase I Dose-Escalation Study of Safety, Efficacy, and Immune Activation (PIVOT-02)

patient_population: PIVOT-02 melanoma and RCC biomarker subset
change_magnitude: Ratio shift reported; Treg expanded but effector expansion larger
notes: Gogas 2024 — PIVOT-02 biomarker re-analysis. Included via ratio-shift path.
title: Baseline biomarkers of efficacy and on-treatment immune-profile changes associated with bempegaldesleukin plus nivolumab

change_magnitude: Minimal Treg expansion; CD8/NK expansion robust
notes: Vaishampayan 2024 — ARTISTRY-1 Part A monotherapy; nemva designed to AVOID Treg expansion; included via ratio-shift path.
title: Nemvaleukin alfa, a modified interleukin-2 cytokine, as monotherapy and with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced solid tumors (ARTISTRY-1)

change_magnitude: Minimal Treg expansion in combo arm
notes: ARTISTRY-1 Part C combo; separate row for combo cohort.
title: Nemvaleukin alfa, a modified interleukin-2 cytokine, as monotherapy and with pembrolizumab in patients with advanced solid tumors (ARTISTRY-1)

change_magnitude: CD8/NK expansion; minimal Treg changes
notes: Calvo 2025 — ARTISTRY-1 Part B; nemva in post-ICI melanoma and RCC.
title: Nemvaleukin alfa monotherapy in patients with advanced melanoma and renal cell carcinoma: results from the phase 1/2 non-randomized ARTISTRY-1 trial

change_magnitude: Minimal Treg expansion across dosing schedules
notes: Piha-Paul 2025 — ARTISTRY-3 less-frequent IV dosing.
title: Less frequent intravenous dosing of nemvaleukin alfa in patients with advanced solid tumors: the phase 1/2 ARTISTRY-3 trial

intervention: PORTER platform: multi-arm including ICI combinations
change_magnitude: Arm-specific immune changes
notes: Galsky 2025 PORTER — multi-cohort platform; Treg PD a correlative objective. Full-text needed for arm-specific numeric values.
title: Clinical and Translational Results from PORTER, a Multicohort Phase I Platform Trial of Combination Immunotherapy in Metastatic Castration-Resistant Prostate Cancer

change_magnitude: Inconsistent decreases in Tregs; CD8 increases in combo arm
notes: Piha-Paul 2021 JITC — GWN323 phase I; Treg PD inconsistent. Classic anti-GITR human mixed result.
title: First-in-human phase I/Ib open-label dose-escalation study of GWN323 (anti-GITR) as a single agent and in combination with spartalizumab (anti-PD-1) in patients with advanced solid tumors and lymphomas

intervention: TRX518 (anti-GITR) single agent or combo (gemcitabine / pembrolizumab / nivolumab)
change_magnitude: Kinetics varied by combo regimen
notes: Davar 2022 — TRX518 phase IB; paired PBMC and tumor Treg PD; combo-regimen-dependent.
title: Phase IB Study of GITR Agonist Antibody TRX518 Singly and in Combination with Gemcitabine, Pembrolizumab, or Nivolumab in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors

change_magnitude: Intratumoral Treg kinetics differed by combo regimen
notes: Tumor-compartment row for Davar 2022. Sample size (20) is estimate from paired biopsies per paper.
title: Phase IB Study of GITR Agonist Antibody TRX518 Singly and in Combination with Gemcitabine, Pembrolizumab, or Nivolumab in Patients with Advanced Solid Tumors
